A_single_pass_warp_q6k.ambiguous_removable_pct: up to +q6k_gemv bw gap if coalescing improves
A_single_pass_warp_q6k.correctness_risk: low (dequant unchanged; only reduce topology changes)
A_single_pass_warp_q6k.performance_risk: medium (warp-lane mapping of the 16 pos-axis; LDS vs shuffle)

kernels_replaced: external r_* sum (incl. lm_head r_32_4_1187 + n1)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.ambiguous_removable_pct: q6k_gemv 503->650 bw gap (~3% if coalescing matches G3)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.expected_WD_gain: TIER_B floor ~3.5% (reduce+partial-traffic elimination, clean mechanism) -> TIER_A ~7.0% (if bw gap closes)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.implementation_complexity: medium-low (reuse Q6_K coop dequant body + swap partials-write for in-warp reduce, exactly as Q4_K G3 does over its lane map)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.correctness_risk: low (dequant + dot byte-identical; only the cross-pos reduction moves in-kernel)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.performance_risk: medium (map pos(16) to lidx0 warp lane + LanePartition(extent=16); 16<warp32 so a half-warp partition reduce)
B_q6k_lanemap_g3_like.why_selected: minimal, code-grounded: the Q6_K coop body ALREADY has pos=16 as a LOCAL lane axis with coalesced loads; the ONLY change is replacing the per-lane partials[row,pos] write + external .sum(axis=1) with the existing quant-agnostic lane_partition_reduce_sum over the 16 pos-lanes, storing out[row] directly -- the exact shape of the proven Q4_K G3 win. lm_head folds in (same route, shape-guarded). All primitives exist.

C_two_stage_less_reduce_q6k.note: keep partials but fuse/shrink the r_* reduce
C_two_stage_less_reduce_q6k.why_not: smaller win; still pays partials-buffer traffic; B subsumes it

D_lm_head_folded_direct.note: LH0: standalone lm_head is TIER_B and not preferred; fold its r_32_4_1187 reduce into B via the out>=100000 shape guard
